table_description: Individual agent user accounts for portal access. PK is login_id (not system_id).

table_columns.name: login_id
table_columns.description: PK. Unique login username (e.g. acmea1, acmegroup).

table_columns.name: system_id
table_columns.description: Shared system user ID (multiple logins may share one).

sample_queries.question: List all active agent logins
sample_queries.sql: SELECT login_id, first_name, last_name, email FROM agent_logins WHERE status = 'Active'

sample_queries.question: Which customers belong to agent acmea1?
sample_queries.sql: SELECT DISTINCT ppa.customer_ref, c.index_name FROM agent_logins al JOIN provider_policy_access ppa ON al.provider_ref = ppa.provider_ref LEFT JOIN customers c ON ppa.customer_ref = c.system_id WHERE al.login_id = 'acmea1'

sample_queries.question: How many active policies does acmegroup have?
sample_queries.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS active_policies FROM agent_logins al JOIN provider_policy_access ppa ON al.provider_ref = ppa.provider_ref JOIN policies p ON ppa.policy_system_id = p.system_id WHERE al.login_id = 'acmegroup' AND p.status = 'Active'

sample_queries.question: Total premium for active policies under an agent
sample_queries.sql: SELECT SUM(p.full_term_amt) AS total_premium FROM agent_logins al JOIN provider_policy_access ppa ON al.provider_ref = ppa.provider_ref JOIN policies p ON ppa.policy_system_id = p.system_id WHERE al.login_id = 'acmegroup' AND p.status = 'Active'

rules: CRITICAL: To find policies, customers, vehicles, or ANY data for an agent, you MUST use provider_policy_access as the bridge.
Join chain: agent_logins.provider_ref = provider_policy_access.provider_ref, then provider_policy_access.policy_system_id = policies.system_id.
NEVER join agent_logins.provider_ref directly to policies.provider_ref — this WILL return 0 rows.
NEVER use providers.index_name to find agent policies — agents and providers have different system_ids.
